entry.title: New: RES26-168 (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
GRANTING A SPECIAL MANAGEMENT AREA MAJOR PERMIT TO ALLOW FOR THE CONSTRUCTION OF A NEW COMMERCIAL BUILDING WITH A PARKING LOT, CARETAKER DWELLING, AND ASSOCIATED IMPROVEMENTS IN NĀNĀKULI, OʻAHU.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: Bill 172 (Hawaiʻi County) — On agenda 2026-07-22
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 26-44, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2027

Appropriates revenues in the revenues in the State Motor Vehicle Weight Tax account ($6,282.72) and appropriates the same to the Vehicle Registration Other Current Expenses account. Funds will be used for expenses associated with programming and updating the County’s motor vehicle registration system.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-22

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 162 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 3 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES

Adds a new article to establish signage requirements for paid parking facilities, to include a schedule of rates, notice of any additional fees, hours of operation, and information on towing procedures.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-07-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 154 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-05-04
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO COMMERCIAL PARKING LOTS AND GARAGES

Removes commercial parking lots and garages as a permitted use in the general, village commercial, industrial-commercial mixed, limited industrial, general industrial, and Downtown Hilo commercial zoning districts.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-04

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 146 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the State Grants – Transit-Oriented Development Capital Improvement Program for 34 Rainbow Affordable Housing Project account ($1,000,000)

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 131 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-034:045 (Applicant: CU

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to construct a 41-stall parking lot for the proposed new credit union facility to be located on Tax Map Key: 2-2-034:036. The property is located at 494 Hīnano Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 130 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-44, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2005 EDITION), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA HOUSELOTS, WAIĀKEA SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to change of zone amendment for a ten-year extension to Condition C (Complete Construction), amendment to Condition D (Dedication of Road Widening), and deletion of Condition E (Roadway Improvements) which would allow the applicant to construct a new credit union branch facility. The property is located at 533 Manono Street. R

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 137 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-22 (PUNA DISTRICT ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 20 ACRES (A-20a) TO FAMILY AGRICULTURAL – 1 ACRE (FA-1a) AT ʻŌLAʻA, PUNA, HAWAI‘I, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 1-7-016:010 (Applicant

Benjamin and Olimpia Love) (Area: 5.79 Acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into five lots. The property is located at 17-4050 Kuaʻāina Road.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 142 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 14 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY ADDING A NEW ARTICLE RELATING TO THE ESTABLISHMENT OF AN ADOPT-A-COUNTY-STREET PROGRAM

Establishes an Adopt-A-County-Street program in partnership with the Department of Public Works to enable volunteers to assist in maintaining clean and safe County streets, roadways, and right-of-ways.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 132 (Hawaiʻi County): On agenda 2026-05-19
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 6 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED) BY ADDING AN ARTICLE RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES IN KAILUA VILLAGE

Adds a new article to regulate parking rates at private parking facilities in Kailua Village.

Latest action: On agenda 2026-05-19

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 129 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 10, DIVISION 5, SECTION 24-280, SCHEDULE 28 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO NO PARKING AT ANYTIME

Prohibits parking at any time for certain portions of Ali‘i Drive fronting Kahalu‘u Beach Park.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-04-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 121 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 09-159, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – 5 ACRES (A-5a) TO MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 30,000 SQUARE FEET (RM-30) AT KEAUHOU, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-8-010:101 (Applicant

Kona Country Club, Inc.) (Area: 51.058 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested ten-year time extension to Condition D (complete construction). The property is located mauka of the Māmalahoa Highway Bypass, between the highway and the Kona Country Club mauka golf course.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 120 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 04-149, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM AGRICULTURAL – FIVE ACRES (A-5a) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 20,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-20) AT KALAOA 1ST AND 2ND, NORTH KONA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 7-3-011:068 (Applicant

Ernest and Elizabeth Young) (Area: 1.86 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested deletion of Condition H, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property, and to amend Condition C for a five-year time extension to secure final subdivision approval. The property is located at the southwest corner of the intersection of Mahilani Drive and Māmalahoa Highway.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 119 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 02-93, WHICH AMENDED SECTION 25-8-7 (NORTH AND SOUTH KOHALA DISTRICTS ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL (A-5a) TO RESIDENTIAL AND AGRICULTURAL (RA-2a) AT ‘ŌULI, SOUTH KOHALA, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 6-2-007:002 (Applicant

Clemson and Janet Lam) (Area: 5 acres) The Leeward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested amendment to Condition D, which prohibits construction of a second dwelling unit on the property. The property is located at 62-2039 Kawaihae Road.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 118 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – Hawaiʻi County Police Department Pedestrian and Bicycle Safety Grant account ($54,098) and appropriates the same to the Hawaiʻi County Police Department Pedestrian and Bicycle Safety Program account.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 116 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – Hawaiʻi County Police Department 2023 Selective Traffic Enforcement Program Grant account ($54,814) and appropriates the same to the Hawaiʻi County Police Department 2023 Selective Traffic Enforcement Program account.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-02-18

entry.title: Bill 111 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP) ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 3 ACRES (A-3a) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-4-080:014 (Applicant

Mailani Development, LLC) (Area: 4.4669 acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into 14 house lots and a road lot. The property is located at 1077 Ahe Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-01-23

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 109 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 10, DIVISION 5, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO NO PARKING AT ANYTIME

Prohibits parking at any time on Hina-Lani Street, from Queen Ka‘ahumanu Highway to Anini Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2026-01-07

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 101 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-46, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND THE FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Appropriates excess fuel tax revenues collected from fiscal years 2022-2023 and 2023-2024 to the following projects totaling $1,493,554.75: Roadway Projects ($448,066.43), Bridge Inspection/Repair ($298,710.95), and Local Road Projects ($746,777.37). Funds for these projects shall be provided from fuel tax revenues, in excess of what was projected in Ordinance No. 23-18.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-11-19

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 104 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the Federal Grants – Traffic Safety Training Project Grant account ($18,440)

entry.content:

entry.title: Bill 92 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-46, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2025, TO JUNE 30, 2026

Increases the Public Works Mohouli Street Rehabilitation Project ($635,000) and the Mohouli Street Rehabilitation – Federal Project ($2,550,000) to the Capital Budget. Funds for these projects shall be provided from General Obligation Bonds, Capital Projects Fund – Fund Balance and/or other Sources ($635,000) and Federal Grants Receivable ($2,550,000).

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-11-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 84 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 10, DIVISION 7, SECTION 24-292, SCHEDULE 40, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PARKING METER ZONES

Repeals the listing in subsection (c) relating to the eight-hour parking meter zone at the Aupuni Center parking lot.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-11-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 88 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 10-32, AS AMENDED, WHICH RECLASSIFIED LANDS FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 7,500 SQUARE FEET (RS-7.5) AND MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 1,000 SQUARE FEET (RM-1) TO MULTIPLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 1,500 SQUARE FEET (RM-1.5) AT KŪKŪAU 1ST, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEYS: 2-4-025:048, 053, AND 080. (Applicant

Ikaika Ohana) (Area: 5.05 Acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this request to amend Condition D (Complete Construction), which would allow the applicant to start construction no later than January 2026, with an estimated completion timeline of 18-24 months. The property is located at 350 Kapiʻolani Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-11-05

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 82 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 9, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PEDESTRIANS

Prohibits pedestrians from entering a roadway, obstructing traffic, or approaching moving vehicles for business and employment purposes.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-10-22

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 75 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
REPEALS SECTION 5 OF ORDINANCE NO. 22-94 AND AMENDS CHAPTER 18, ARTICLE 4, DIVISION 1, SECTION 18-90, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO FARES FOR PUBLIC TRANSPORTATION

Extends the temporary suspension of fares for all mass transit riders through December 31, 2028. The fare suspension will not apply to County-contracted shared-ride services or curb-to-curb van services operated by the Department of Parks and Recreation.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-10-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 73 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE ZONE MAP CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL - 15,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-15) TO SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL - 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-4-011:153 (Applicant

Douglas and Shelley Uyeda) (Area: 21,778 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property into two lots and separate the two existing single-family dwellings currently on the property, enabling each dwelling to exist on its own lot. The property is located at 461 Kehaulani Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-09-03

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 67 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP) ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE ZONE MAP CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL -10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO GENERAL COMMERCIAL-10,000 SQUARE FEET (CG-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-022:022 (Applicant

Correa Ohana, LLC) (Area: 33,180 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for the requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to demolish the existing single-family dwelling and garage and construct a multi-family apartment complex. The property is located at 1198 Kinoʻole Street.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-09-03

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 62 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 24-32, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET FOR THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2025

entry.title: Bill 61 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 24-32, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET FOR THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2025

Appropriates revenues in the Federal Grants – County of Hawai‘i Smart and Safe Intersection Demonstration account ($3,360,000)

Status: Adopted → —

entry.title: Bill 38 (Hawaiʻi County): Passed Second Reading → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 19, ARTICLE 13, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO REAL PROPERTY TAX CREDITS

Establishes a Private Road Tax Credit of up to $500 to be applied towards a homeowner’s real property tax bill when the homeowner expends at least $100 annually to maintain, repair, or improve a private roadway that is not restricted by a gate and is managed by a road maintenance organization.

Status: Passed Second Reading → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-10-08

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 59 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 24, ARTICLE 9, OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PEDESTRIANS

Prohibits certain acts by pedestrians as follows: “Receiving or soliciting rides for employment or business unless legally parked; if convicted of a crime within one year of a previous violation; standing, watching or guarding of a vehicle while parked on a street or highway.”

Status: Adopted → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-07-09

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 40 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE ZONE MAP CLASSIFICATION FROM AGRICULTURAL – 10 ACRES (A-10a) TO AGRICULTURAL – 5 ACRES (A-5a) AT PANAʻEWA, HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-048:013 (POR.) (Applicant

Hoʻoulu-Lāhui) (Area: approx. 12.043 acres) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to subdivide the property that currently houses Kua O Ka Lā New Century Public Charter School and enable the school to transition from a lease agreement to fee-simple ownership. The property is located at 345 Makalika Street.

Status: Adopted → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-05-21

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 46 (Hawaiʻi County): Failed → —
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-30 (NĀʻĀLEHU ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE ZONE MAP CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE FAMILY RESIDENTIAL 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO VILLAGE COMMERCIAL 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CV-10) AT NĀʻĀLEHU, KAʻŪ, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 9-5-009:001 (POR.) (Applicant: L.A

Services Inc.) (Area: approx. 6,920.3 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its unfavorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to establish a unified zoning designation for the entire property. The property is located at 95-5591 Māmalahoa Highway.

Status: Failed → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-05-21

entry.title: Bill 54 (Hawaiʻi County): Adopted → —
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 24-33, AS AMENDED, RELATING TO PUBLIC IMPROVEMENTS AND FINANCING THEREOF FOR THE FISCAL YEAR JULY 1, 2024 TO JUNE 30, 2025

Appropriates excess fuel tax revenues collected from fiscal years 2022-2023 and 2023-2024 to the following projects totaling $1,493,554.75: Roadway Projects ($448,066.43), Bridge Inspection/Repair ($298,710.95), and Local Road Projects ($746,777.37). Funds for these projects shall be provided from fuel tax revenues, in excess of what was projected in Ordinance No. 23-18.

Status: Adopted → —

Latest action: On agenda 2025-06-18

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL041(26) (Honolulu): PUBLISH → ITT
entry.content:
RELATING TO TRANSPORTATION.

Conforms ROH provisions to House Bill 2021, HD2, SD2, CD1, Relating to Transportation. Adds definitions for “Electric Bicycle,” “Class 1 Electric Bicycle,” Class 2 Electric Bicycle,” Class 3 Electric Bicycle, “Electric Micro-mobility Device, “High-speed Electric Device,” and “Electric Motorcycle.” Substitutes the term “electric bicycle” for the terms “bicycle with motor,” “motorized bicycles,” and similar terms. Substitutes the term “motor-driven cycle” for the term “motor scooter.” Regulates riding electric bicycles on sidewalks under certain circumstances. Prohibits on bikeways: (1) driving a vehicle, other than a bicycle, electric foot scooter, or electric micro-mobility device, with specified exceptions; and (2) operating mopeds or electric motorcycles. Prohibits a person under the age of sixteen from operating a class 2 or class 3 electric bicycle without direct supervision. Repeals as preempted: (1) certain safe driving requirements for the operation of electric bicycles on public streets, paths, or tra

Status: PUBLISH → ITT

Latest action: CR-203(26) Reported out for passage on third reading as amended in CD1 form.

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL033(26) (Honolulu): CCL/PH → BUD
entry.content:
RELATING TO FUNDS.

Establishes a special fund to be called the Parks Maintenance and Recreation Services Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds generated by the DPR relating to the use of lands dedicated for park use or for park maintenance and recreation services, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of beaches and parks under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for DPR operations, parks maintenance and recreational facilities, and programs under the jurisdiction of the DPR. Establishes a special fund to be called the Botanical Gardens and Urban Forestry Revolving Fund for the deposit of all revenues and proceeds relating to the use of lands dedicated for botanical gardens, community gardens, and urban forestry programs and operations generated by the DPR, or otherwise earmarked for the restoration, operation, or maintenance of gardens and park and street trees under the jurisdiction of the DPR, to be expended for the operations of the DPR's Division of Urban Forest

Status: CCL/PH → BUD

Latest action: See: Proposed draft version OCS2026-0422/7/7/2026 1:16 PM Amended to CD2 and postponed to a date and time to be determined by the Committee Chair.

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL041(26) (Honolulu): CCL/PH → PUBLISH
entry.content:
RELATING TO TRANSPORTATION.

Conforms ROH provisions to House Bill 2021, HD2, SD2, CD1, Relating to Transportation. Adds definitions for “Electric Bicycle,” “Class 1 Electric Bicycle,” Class 2 Electric Bicycle,” Class 3 Electric Bicycle, “Electric Micro-mobility Device, “High-speed Electric Device,” and “Electric Motorcycle.” Substitutes the term “electric bicycle” for the terms “bicycle with motor,” “motorized bicycles,” and similar terms. Substitutes the term “motor-driven cycle” for the term “motor scooter.” Regulates riding electric bicycles on sidewalks under certain circumstances. Prohibits on bikeways: (1) driving a vehicle, other than a bicycle, electric foot scooter, or electric micro-mobility device, with specified exceptions; and (2) operating mopeds or electric motorcycles. Prohibits a person under the age of sixteen from operating a class 2 or class 3 electric bicycle without direct supervision. Repeals as preempted: (1) certain safe driving requirements for the operation of electric bicycles on public streets, paths, or tra

Status: CCL/PH → PUBLISH

Latest action: Second reading notice published in the Honolulu Star-Advertiser.

View on the council site

entry.title: BILL047(26) (Honolulu): CCL → BUD
entry.content:
REALTING TO THE MOTOR VEHICLE WEIGHT TAX.

Reduces the vehicle weight tax rates for motor vehicles designed primarily for carrying passengers; trucks and noncommercial vehicles having a net weight of 6,500 pounds or less; and vehicles designed for carrying property or for purposes other than carrying passengers. Takes effect on July 1, 2027.

Status: CCL → BUD

Latest action: CR-196(26) Reported out for passage on second reading and scheduling of a public hearing.

View on the council site

entry.title: New: BILL051(26) (Honolulu) — INTRO
entry.content:
RELATING TO PARKING.

Requires the DTS Director to mark with a warning certain parking restricted areas along curbs within one-quarter mile of any public access to a hiking trail.

Latest action: Introduced.

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 162 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 3 OF THE HAWAI‘I COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO PAID PARKING FACILITIES

Adds a new article to establish signage requirements for paid parking facilities, to include a schedule of rates, notice of any additional fees, hours of operation, and information on towing procedures.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 162 passes second & final reading - 07/08/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 154 (Hawaiʻi County): PCPLUED - Referred to the Planning Director and The Leeward and Windward Planning Commissions - 05/04/26
entry.content:
AMENDS CHAPTER 25, ARTICLES 1, 5, AND 7, OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), RELATING TO COMMERCIAL PARKING LOTS AND GARAGES

Removes commercial parking lots and garages as a permitted use in the general, village commercial, industrial-commercial mixed, limited industrial, general industrial, and Downtown Hilo commercial zoning districts.

Latest action: PCPLUED - Referred to the Planning Director and The Leeward and Windward Planning Commissions - 05/04/26

View on the council site

entry.title: Bill 146 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS ORDINANCE NO. 25-45, AS AMENDED, THE OPERATING BUDGET OF THE COUNTY OF HAWAIʻI FOR THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING JUNE 30, 2026

Increases revenues in the State Grants – Transit-Oriented Development Capital Improvement Program for 34 Rainbow Affordable Housing Project account ($1,000,000)

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 146 passes second & final reading - 05/05/26

entry.title: Bill 131 (Hawaiʻi County): — → Passed Second Reading
entry.content:
AMENDS SECTION 25-8-33 (CITY OF HILO ZONE MAP), ARTICLE 8, CHAPTER 25 (ZONING CODE) OF THE HAWAIʻI COUNTY CODE 1983 (2016 EDITION, AS AMENDED), BY CHANGING THE DISTRICT CLASSIFICATION FROM SINGLE-FAMILY RESIDENTIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (RS-10) TO NEIGHBORHOOD COMMERCIAL – 10,000 SQUARE FEET (CN-10) AT WAIĀKEA, SOUTH HILO, HAWAIʻI, COVERED BY TAX MAP KEY: 2-2-034:045 (Applicant: CU

Hawaiʻi Federal Credit Union) (Area: 22,500 square feet) The Windward Planning Commission forwards its favorable recommendation for this requested change of zone, which would allow the applicant to construct a 41-stall parking lot for the proposed new credit union facility to be located on Tax Map Key: 2-2-034:036. The property is located at 494 Hīnano Street.

Status: — → Passed Second Reading

Latest action: Council: Bill 131 passes second & final reading - 04/08/2026

View on the council site
